content: Define the TopModule with its inputs and outputs. Include clk, load, ena, data as inputs and q as output.
source:
input  clk
input  load
input  ena  (2 bits)
input  data (100 bits)
output q    (100 bits)

content: Implement a 100-bit register to store the input data when the load signal is active. This register should be updated on the positive edge of clk and reset to zero when load is inactive.
source:
input  clk
input  load
input  data (100 bits)

content: Implement the logic to determine the direction of rotation based on the ena signal. Use minimal combinational logic to decide whether to shift left or right.

Implement the rotation logic that shifts the bits of the register either left or right based on the direction determined by the ena signal. Ensure that the rotated bits wrap around to the other end of the register.
source:
input  ena  (2 bits)
output q    (100 bits)

content: Connect the output of the rotation logic to the output port q. This connection should be direct without any additional registers to minimize hardware usage.
